ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the test case that generated this result */,
  "execution_date" TEXT /* Date when the test was executed */,
  "actual_status" INTEGER /* The actual HTTP status received during testing */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Any error message logged during the test */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the test result was recorded */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who executed the test */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Time taken for the server to respond to the test request */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., functional, performance, security) */,
  "test_duration" REAL /* Duration of the test execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "url_replacements" (
  "replacement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the replacement entry */,
  "replacement_string" TEXT /* String used for replacing spaces in URLs */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the replacement string's purpose */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the replacement entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the replacement entry */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the replacement entry */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last modified the replacement entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("replacement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, tester, viewer) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user account */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the user accessed the platform */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., start test, view results) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the access log entry was created */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to access the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being scheduled */,
  "scheduled_date" TEXT /* Date when the test is scheduled to run */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the test (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the schedule entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the schedule entry */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the schedule entry */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last modified the schedule entry */,
  "next_scheduled_date" TEXT /* Next date when the test is scheduled to run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., error, warning, info) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the alert was created */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who resolved the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_run_details" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test run */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being run */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the test run started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the test run ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test run (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the test run was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who initiated the test run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id")
)
